page: Amazon Web Services r6a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture utilizing an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 3.6 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across multiple vectors. The instance achieves top 25% results in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as Passmark CPU and memory scores. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is highly efficient, placing in the top 25%, while large block read and write operations perform in the average tier. In database and key-value operations, the server demonstrates strong capabilities, highlighted by high throughput in Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests. It also shows top 10% performance in text generation for small language models, though performance scales down to average for larger models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6a.16xlarge offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for workloads requiring large in-memory footprints without the premium of GPU hardware. The absence of local NVMe storage means storage performance is dependent on network-attached volumes. This server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale databases, in-memory caches like Redis, software compilation, and data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores) running at 3.6 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. The instance is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, delivering a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the top 25% for multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth for small cached blocks, and database operations. It is highly suited for memory-bound applications, including large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching, software compilation, and medium-scale data processing pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, designed for high-performance database and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.16xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and AMD EPYC processors for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring high-density DDR4 RAM and dedicated AMD EPYC processors for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC 7R13 at 3.6 GHz
512.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Strong benchmark performance in database operations and Redis caching
Designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage requirements
